behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-to-text model output quality (speaker diarization fidelity). A stubbed transcript would hide diarization failures. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets preservation of speaker labels at STT output, not a required multi-step consistency check across multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real audio through the real STT API and verify returned transcript includes correct speaker labels for utterances; then confirm those labels are available to the summarization input contract.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local module handoff/parsing-to-structure wiring. The failure appears when parser output is mapped into the summary input schema across module boundaries. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about parsing structured assignments from transcript text, not judging model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript text fixtures through the real parser + schema-mapper modules together and assert extracted assignments contain both assignee and task text in the summary input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real LLM output content quality (whether it includes required action items per attendee). Mocked LLM responses would mask this. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text does not explicitly require validating cross-step consistency over multiple model-dependent stages; it can be validated at the live summary generation step with real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the live summary.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is routing/schema placement logic across internal modules (extraction output -> summary schema). No explicit requirement for real external infra or model-quality judgment in this behavior text. Rejected Agentic because it focuses on where already-identified decisions are routed, not whether a model correctly identifies them.
behaviors.test_description: Run decision extraction output through the real routing/serialization path and assert items land in `decisions` schema fields and are absent from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real LLM decision extraction/generation behavior from transcript content. Deterministic stubs cannot validate whether the model surfaces decisions correctly. Rejected Workflow because the behavior text can be satisfied by single-step transcript-to-summary model behavior without explicitly requiring multi-step agentic state chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts containing explicit decisions, run real summarization, and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and not only in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly chains multiple model-dependent steps (segment STT/summarization over sequence, then merge) with cross-step consistency requirements for final coherence. Single-step Agentic tests miss sequence and state-carryover failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through segmentation, sequential real processing per segment, and merge; verify final summary remains coherent and preserves segment-level action items/decisions without duplication or loss.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage agentic pipeline with real STT and LLM steps in sequence. Success depends on chained model outputs and orchestration across stages, so lower levels would give false confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full production-like flow on a real meeting audio file: upload, real STT, real summarization, and final structured output delivery; assert completion and schema-valid output without manual repair.
